Get in Touch
VU Amsterdam Research Lab

Co-creating knowledge for a more just society

Bringing together communities, researchers, and lived experiences to build knowledge that truly includes everyone.

Explore our work How we do it
Our Approach

What we do

We believe knowledge is richer when it comes from many places. Our lab bridges academia and lived experience through three core commitments.

Co-create research

We design and conduct research together with communities, not about them. Participants shape questions, methods, and findings from the very beginning.

Develop inclusive methodologies

We rethink research methods to make them accessible, participatory, and culturally responsive — from storytelling circles to visual research tools.

Connect academic & experiential knowledge

We create spaces where scholarly research and the knowledge people carry from their own lives enrich and challenge each other equally.

Purpose

Knowledge should belong to everyone

Structural inequalities shape who gets to produce knowledge, whose stories are told, and whose expertise is recognised. We work to change that — one research partnership at a time.

12+Community partnerships
5Active research themes
40+Co-researchers worldwide
Research Themes

What we explore together

Our research is organised around themes that matter to communities facing exclusion, uncertainty, and the need to be heard.

Theme 01

Forced Migration

Centering the voices and agency of people who have been displaced, and challenging narratives that strip them of complexity.

Theme 02

Youth Beyond Binaries

Exploring how young people navigate identity, gender, and belonging beyond imposed categories — with and for youth communities.

Theme 03

Economic Insecurities

Examining how financial precarity shapes lives, and how communities develop strategies of resilience and collective care.

Theme 04

Storytelling & Identity

Using narrative, art, and creative methods to surface how people understand themselves and their place in the world.

Learning & Teaching

Participatory learning in practice

We design learning experiences where everyone is both teacher and student — because knowledge flows in all directions.

01

Collaborative workshops

Hands-on sessions where academic researchers and community members co-develop tools, frameworks, and findings together.

02

Participatory course design

University courses co-created with the communities they study — students learn directly from lived experience, not just textbooks.

03

Open knowledge sharing

All methodologies, toolkits, and research outputs are made available openly so others can build on our work.

Our Projects

Research in action

Concrete partnerships and inquiries where co-creation happens. Filter by theme to find what resonates with you.

Forced Migration

Engaged Scholarship: Narratives of Change

A comparative project investigating how engaged scholarship can contribute to the societal inclusion of refugees across South Africa, the US, and the Netherlands.

OngoingComparative / 3 continents↗
Forced Migration

The House of Stories (Het Verhalenhuis)

A creative collaboration giving people with refugee backgrounds professional guidance to share their unheard stories on stage — transformed into podcasts and live performances.

OngoingStory Academy × Refugee Academy↗
Forced Migration

2incING Project

Bridging policymakers and refugee/migrant-led organisations across four EU countries to create policies that are genuinely grounded in community realities.

OngoingEU AMIF funded · 5 countries↗
Storytelling & Identity

Common Ground Dialogues

Bringing together people across political and social divides through Storytelling Circles — using fiction and play to address polarising issues with empathy.

Ongoing2023–2025 · Creative Europe↗
Resisting Violence

LIMBO

A workshop series centring queer refugee stories through creative practices, co-creating knowledge about resilience and the role of engaged scholarship in queer refugee lives.

OngoingSep 2024–Jun 2025 · ILGA Europe↗
Youth Beyond Binaries

Leadership & Resilience: Co-Creation with Undocumented Youth

Undocumented youth and VU researchers working together to create more inclusive educational environments — from mental wellbeing to transitions into higher education.

OngoingNWA Route Resilience↗
Resisting Violence

Boundaries in Art-Based Research

A refugee-led project creating space to collectively articulate the discomforts and conditions for meaningful co-creation in art and research about refugee experiences.

OngoingRefugee-led↗
Economic Insecurities

Economic Pathways for Queer Men in Nairobi

Community-led research exploring financial resilience and queer entrepreneurship among financially vulnerable queer men in Nairobi's hustle economies.

OngoingJun 2024–Aug 2025 · Wenner-Gren↗
Youth Beyond Binaries

Trans Healthcare in Den Haag

Mapping the barriers transgender youth face in accessing care in The Hague, integrating youth and professional perspectives towards systemic improvement.

OngoingMunicipality of The Hague↗
Resisting Violence

Magazine for Global Reparations

A community magazine by Aralez presenting a manifesto for reparations, connecting decolonisation activism with academic reflection on the coloniality of Dutch universities.

OngoingAralez × CIK Lab↗
Resisting Violence

Exhibition: Wounded Storytellers

A photo-voice study with 32 gang-violence survivors in Cape Town, co-curating an exhibition that centres their stories of injury, identity transformation, and community healing.

NewExhibition 2025 · Cape Town↗
Resisting Violence

Memory Workshops in Colombia

Co-producing local knowledge about armed conflict with rural Colombian communities using creative memory tools — timelines, memory webs, and emotional biographies.

OngoingColombia↗
Resisting Violence

Climate Mobility: North–South Perspectives

Building a scholar-activist network across Europe and Latin America to address the power dynamics shaping who can and cannot move in the face of climate change.

OngoingNorth–South partnership↗
Youth Beyond Binaries

Library of In-Between Stories

Young people growing up between worlds co-create a digital library of in-between narratives and a masterclass for youth professionals.

OngoingMulti-institution collaboration↗
Economic Insecurities

Consultative Panel on Social Assistance

A participatory think tank bringing together welfare researchers, policymakers, and recipients to make social assistance research more relevant to people's daily lives.

OngoingNetherlands↗
Economic Insecurities

Our Cloud: Data Centre Activists & NGOs

Connecting grassroots data centre resistance movements with digital rights NGOs to co-develop democratic alternatives to corporate digital infrastructure.

OngoingNetherlands + international↗
Economic Insecurities

In Search of an In-Between Space

Co-creative action research building new chains of trust between Amsterdam residents, frontline workers, and policymakers to tackle persistent inequalities.

NewSep 2024 · Amsterdam↗

The Team

The people behind the work

A growing collective of researchers, community scholars, and practitioners. Click a group to meet the people in it, and any name to read more.

Core Researchers
4 members
+
Prof. Halleh Ghorashi Principal Investigator
▾
Professor at Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam and co-creator of the Co-Creation for Inclusive Knowledges Lab. Halleh leads engaged scholarship research on refugee inclusion across South Africa, the US, and the Netherlands, and co-directs projects including Het Verhalenhuis and In Search of an In-Between Space.
Dr. Elena Ponzoni Senior Researcher
▾
Senior researcher and co-creator of multiple lab projects, including the Engaged Scholarship comparative study, Het Verhalenhuis, the 2incING EU project, the Library of In-Between Stories, and In Search of an In-Between Space. Elena's work focuses on participatory knowledge production and the inclusion of marginalised communities.
RP
Dr. Roos Prins Senior Researcher
▾
Works on economic precarity and community resilience, combining ethnographic and participatory methods. Leads the Precarious Platforms and Collective Debt projects.
OA
Dr. Osei Acheampong Senior Researcher
▾
Focuses on postcolonial epistemologies and decolonial research methodologies. Leads the Intergenerational Memory Project and contributes to the Borders of Belonging research.
Researchers
3 members
+
Dr. Lise Woensdregt Researcher
▾
Researcher using community-led research and action (CLRA) methods. Lise leads the Economic Pathways for Queer Men in Nairobi project (Wenner-Gren funded) and co-creates the Common Ground Dialogues and In Search of an In-Between Space projects. Her work centres collaborative anthropology and inclusive research practices.
Timo Korstenbroek Researcher
▾
Researcher on the Common Ground Dialogues project (Creative Europe funded), studying how storytelling circles can bridge social and political divides. Timo observes and contributes to the development of the CGD methodology and its potential to transform public dialogue through artistic practice.
Miriam Ocadiz Researcher
▾
Researcher contributing to the Engaged Scholarship: Narratives of Change comparative study and Het Verhalenhuis (House of Stories). Miriam's work focuses on the role of engaged scholarship in the societal inclusion of refugees, and on storytelling as a tool for amplifying unheard voices.
Community Research Fellows
5 members
+
KV
Kofi Vandermeer Community Research Fellow
▾
Brings expertise in youth participatory action research and oral history practice across West African and European contexts. Co-leads the Archive of Unheard Voices project.
FN
Fatou Ndiaye Community Liaison & Fellow
▾
Builds and sustains relationships with community partners; brings extensive experience in grassroots organising and advocacy in West African diaspora communities in the Netherlands.
YB
Yusra Bashir Community Research Fellow
▾
Co-researcher with lived experience of asylum seeking. Brings community knowledge to the Narrating Displacement and Legal Literacy Circles projects, and trains other community co-researchers.
TV
Theo Vandenberghe Community Research Fellow
▾
Non-binary youth researcher and activist contributing to the Queering the Curriculum and Young & Non-Binary in the Netherlands projects. Specialises in participatory design with young people.
GS
Gladys Sarpong Community Research Fellow
▾
Food justice activist and community organiser in Amsterdam Zuidoost. Leads community engagement for the Food Insecurity Mapping project and facilitates mutual aid research circles.
PhD Researchers
4 members
+
AB
Amara Boateng PhD Researcher
▾
Exploring how forced migrants navigate knowledge systems and reclaim narrative authority in host country contexts. Dissertation: "Epistemic Displacement: Knowledge-making at the Borders of Belonging."
PH
Priya Hussain PhD Researcher
▾
Researching intergenerational transmission of identity among South Asian diaspora youth in the Netherlands, using photo-voice and narrative inquiry as primary methods.
NW
Nils Westerberg PhD Researcher
▾
Examining platform labour and algorithmic precarity among migrant gig workers, contributing to the Precarious Platforms project with ethnographic research.
CA
Chioma Adeyemi PhD Researcher
▾
Investigating how Black feminist epistemologies can transform social science research design, with a focus on community accountability and knowledge repatriation.
Education & Operations
3 members
+
JT
Jonas Timmer Education Coordinator
▾
Designs participatory learning experiences and coordinates community partnership programs at VU Amsterdam. Oversees the co-designed course curriculum and student research partnerships.
MK
Mia Kovalev Lab Manager & Research Assistant
▾
Keeps the lab running smoothly — from project coordination and ethics applications to community workshop facilitation and open knowledge publishing.
EL
Emilia Lopes Communications & Dissemination
▾
Translates research into accessible formats for diverse audiences, manages the lab's public presence, and coordinates open-access publishing of all lab outputs.
From the Field

Spring School 2025

A week of co-creation, dialogue, and shared meals — bringing together researchers, community partners, and practitioners from across Europe and beyond.

Workshop · June 2025

Opening Day — Coming Together

Participants from refugee backgrounds, engaged academics, and community organisations gathered at the start of Spring School 2025, co-hosted with P4Mi. A moment to make introductions, set intentions, and build the trust that makes co-creation possible.

Spring School 2025 Read more
Arts-based · Workshop

Collage as Conversation

Using images, scissors, and found materials, participants created visual narratives of their experiences and futures. Arts-based methods opened doors that words alone couldn't reach.

Spring School 2025 Read more
Dialogue · Circle

Sitting in Circle

A whole-group circle session where participants reflected on questions of inclusion, belonging, and the role of engaged scholarship. No hierarchy — just voices, chairs, and careful listening.

Spring School 2025 Read more
Community · Event

Closing Together at VU

At the end of the week, participants gathered in front of the Amsterdam skyline for a closing moment — a reminder of how much ground had been covered, together.

Spring School 2025 Read more
Community · Meal

Cooking as Co-Creation

Food became a method. Participants cooked together — chopping, stirring, sharing recipes from different parts of the world. A reminder that community is built in small, everyday acts.

Spring School 2025 Read more
Keynote · Presentation

Moving Beyond Symbolic Allyship

A powerful keynote session asked: how do we move beyond symbolic allyship and create real, lasting change led by those directly impacted by displacement? Three speakers brought lived and scholarly perspectives together.

Spring School 2025 Read more
Arts-based · Workshop

Making Meaning Together

Hands busy with paper, glue, and images — participants built individual and collective collages that surfaced questions of identity, belonging, and what inclusion really looks like in practice.

Spring School 2025 Read more
Co-Creation for Inclusive Knowledges Lab

A research lab at Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam, committed to building knowledge that includes, centres, and serves all communities.
